description: Every object in the game world is defined in the RuleBook. Objects have properties that determine how they interact with skills and the environment. If an object has a profile in the RuleBook, it is interactable.
rule: If an object has a RuleBook profile, it can be affected by skills. If it has no profile, it is scenery (cannot be interacted with).

hp.description: Hit points. When reduced to 0, the object is destroyed.
hp.note: Every interactable object has HP. Even a wooden pillar can be destroyed.

weight.description: Mass of the object. Affects push force calculations. Heavier objects require more force to move.
weight.note: Push force = skill.force × caster.weight / target.weight. A 10-force hit on a 2-weight pillar moves it 5 units. On a 20-weight boulder, only 0.5 units.

material.description: The material the object is made of. Affects damage type interactions and visual/audio feedback.

destructible.description: Whether the object can be destroyed by reducing HP to 0.

crate.note: Lightweight wooden crate. 10× lighter than the player. Easy to push around.
